sections.title: What is a Skeleton?
points: A skeleton is the supportive framework of bones or hard tissues in an organism.
It is a living tissue because it grows, heals after a fracture, has a blood supply, and nerves.
Bones are the hardest tissues in the body and need proper feeding to stay healthy.
The primary minerals that make bones strong are Calcium and Phosphorus.

sections.title: 1. Exo-skeleton (Outer Support)
points: Definition: Hard tissues found outside the flesh/body.
Found in: Insects and Arthropods.
Examples: Bees, Cockroaches, Grasshoppers, Crickets, Houseflies, Mosquitoes, Spiders, Scorpions, Crabs, Lobsters, Centipedes, and Millipedes.
Special Case: A Tortoise and a Snail have an exo-skeleton (shell) for protection but have different support for their soft bodies.
Functions: Acts like armor to protect soft internal organs and gives the body shape.

sections.title: Growth: Ecdysis / Moulting
points: Animals with exo-skeletons cannot grow continuously because their 'shell' is rigid.
Ecdysis (Moulting): The process of shedding the old outer layer to form a new, larger one.
This leads to growth in spurts (jerky growth) rather than smooth, continuous growth.

sections.title: 2. Hydrostatic Skeleton (Liquid Support)
points: Definition: The body is supported by liquid under pressure within a thin membrane.
Movement: They use the pressure of the fluid and surrounding muscles to change shape and move.
Examples: Earthworms, Slugs, Snails (the soft part), Maggots, Caterpillars, Jellyfish, and Starfish.

sections.title: 3. Endo-skeleton (Inner Support)
points: Definition: Hard tissues (bones and cartilage) found inside the flesh.
Found in: All Vertebrates (animals with backbones).
Examples: Humans (Man), Cows, Goats, Dogs, Lions, Birds, Fish, Frogs, and Snakes.
Growth: Vertebrates grow continuously and the skeleton increases in size along with the body.

sections.title: General Functions of the Skeleton
points: Shape: Gives the body its specific framework.
Support: Supports the soft parts of the body so we can stand/sit.
Protection: Protects delicate organs (e.g., Skull protects the Brain; Ribcage protects Heart/Lungs).
Blood Manufacture: The Red Bone Marrow produces red and white blood cells.
Movement: Provides surfaces for Muscles to attach at the joints.
Storage: Acts as a bank for minerals like Calcium and Phosphorus.

sections.text: Remember: Sharks and Rays have skeletons made of Cartilage, not hard bone! Also, Ossification is the process where cartilage turns into hard bone as we grow.

cards.fact: The periodic shedding of the exoskeleton to allow the animal to grow.
cards.mnemonic: Think of 'Exit' - The insect exits its old skin to grow bigger!

cards.fact: The biological process where cartilage is replaced by hard bone.
Bones grow, heal, and have blood vessels and nerves.
